phase: Phase 7 Flood Constraint map marker UX
goal: Make FEMA flood constraint markers understandable and interactive without changing PostGIS, flood overlay logic, forecasting, school constraints, or SceneView architecture.

marker_styling.size_logic: Marker size and vertical offset scale by the stronger of flood_constraint_score or percent_parcel_constrained.

marker_interaction.custom_card: A movable flood constraint information card is shown on marker click.
marker_interaction.selected_parcel_flow: Flood marker click still dispatches the existing parcel inspect event so selected parcel card, selected parcel flood panel, map focus, and parcel boundary/cage highlight reuse the standard parcel workflow.

layer_registry.severity_counts: Displays live severe/high/moderate counts when the layer is ready.
layer_registry.instruction_text: Click a marker to inspect parcel flood constraints.

marker_click_visual_test.reason: The in-app browser viewport was only 319px wide and exposed the right panel reliably, but not a usable SceneView marker target for a canvas click. The click handler code path was implemented and inspected, and the layer rendered markers from the live API.
marker_click_visual_test.manual_follow_up: Open the app in a wider browser window, toggle Flood Constraints, and click a marker to verify the custom flood info card appears next to the standard selected parcel flow.

validation.build_note: The first build attempts timed out while the Next dev server was also running. After stopping the dev server, npm run build -- --webpack completed successfully, and the dev server was restarted.

remaining_flood_ui_gaps: Manual wide-viewport marker click verification should be performed in the visible app.
Flood layer filters can later expose severity/buildability controls.
Selected parcel flood geometry display remains future work.
Flood constraints are still rendered as high-review markers, not parcel polygon flood overlays.
